primary_detection.name: Misleading Variable and Function Names
primary_detection.description: Uses security-sounding names for malicious functions to create false trust
evidence: validate_input() - Sends data to external server
sanitize_data() - Reads sensitive files without sanitization

underlying_threats.name: Semantic Deception
underlying_threats.description: Function names deliberately mislead about true functionality

underlying_threats.name: Multi-Target Exfiltration
underlying_threats.description: Targets passwd, shadow, and SSH directories

underlying_threats.name: Shell Command Injection
underlying_threats.description: Uses os.system with file paths in shell command

underlying_threats.name: Auto-Execution on Import
underlying_threats.description: security_check() and protect_system() called at load

obfuscation_techniques.purpose: Make malicious code appear to be security tooling
obfuscation_techniques.effectiveness: Very High - exploits trust in security terminology

obfuscation_techniques.purpose: Docstrings describe opposite of actual behavior

verdict_reasoning: This sample uses social engineering at the code level - exploiting the trust developers place in security-named functions. The semantic inversion (security_check actually stealing, sanitize actually reading) represents a sophisticated deception technique.
recommended_actions: Implement semantic analysis comparing function names to behavior
Flag security-themed function names for extra scrutiny
Require documentation verification against actual code behavior
